channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Peptides get their regulatory close-up
item.description: A panel of advisers to the Food and Drug Administration will meet this week to make recommendations on whether specialized pharmacies should be allowed to make some popular wellness peptides.
item.content:encoded:
A panel of advisers to the Food and Drug Administration will meet this week to make recommendations on whether specialized pharmacies should be allowed to make some popular wellness peptides.

(Image credit: Javier Arciga)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
item.description: Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.
item.content:encoded:
Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.

(Image credit: Ramon Dompor for Tradeoffs)
item.dc:creator: Julie Wernau

item.title: CDC director nominee Erica Schwartz says she hopes to shift the agency's tone
item.description: Public health officials acknowledge they need to shed paternalistic messaging if they want to reach more people. The nominee to run the CDC says she will strike a new tone.
item.content:encoded: Public health officials acknowledge they need to shed paternalistic messaging if they want to reach more people. The nominee to run the CDC says she will strike a new tone.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Staying in a job for the health insurance? About 1 in 4 Americans do, a survey says
item.description: About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
About 24% of U.S. employees stay in unwanted jobs just for health insurance, a report out Wednesday finds. Up significantly since 2021, the uptick in "job lock" is a concerning sign for the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: Creative Images Lab)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: How to make the complex world of skincare easy
item.description: NPR's Life Kit breaks down what you really need to know about skincare.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Life Kit breaks down what you really need to know about skincare.

item.title: What's behind the push to get more men on testosterone?
item.description: Testosterone is getting a lot of attention lately. The military just announced it will screen service members for low testosterone. Meanwhile, the Trump administration wants to expand access.
item.content:encoded: Testosterone is getting a lot of attention lately. The military just announced it will screen service members for low testosterone. Meanwhile, the Trump administration wants to expand access.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: What to know about the record cyclospora outbreak
item.description: Confusion about the source of the outbreak and uncertainty about when it might be contained puts the onus on consumers to protect themselves.
item.content:encoded: Confusion about the source of the outbreak and uncertainty about when it might be contained puts the onus on consumers to protect themselves.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: Is that food really made with 100% avocado oil? Likely not
item.description: A new study finds that consumers who are paying a premium for foods advertised as made with avocado oil are probably not getting what they think they're buying.
item.content:encoded:
A new study finds that consumers who are paying a premium for foods advertised as made with avocado oil are probably not getting what they think they're buying.

(Image credit: fcafotodigital)
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: Amid nurse shortage, a university rolls out the welcome mat for men
item.description: Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.
item.content:encoded:
Only about 1 in 8 nurses are men, but their share is growing. A strong job market for nurses and sustained efforts to recruit men into the field have made a difference, including in the South.

(Image credit: Andi Rice for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: FDA still focused on Taylor Farms lettuce as Cyclospora source despite retracted test
item.description: The FDA retracted its only positive test for cyclospora, the parasite at the center of a multistate outbreak, on Taylor Farms lettuce. But the agency believes lettuce from the brand is still linked.
item.content:encoded: The FDA retracted its only positive test for cyclospora, the parasite at the center of a multistate outbreak, on Taylor Farms lettuce. But the agency believes lettuce from the brand is still linked.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi
